title: AI Builders Digest
subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天的主线是 Microsoft Build 之后,大家突然在讲同一件事:harness。Satya 在 No Priors 把这层意思说得很直白——真正的护城河不是哪个模型,而是你怎么把模型、数据、工具、eval 装进一个 harness 里,让自己能在 frontier 上持续 hill climb。Steipete 在 Build 上演讲的题目就叫[build the thing that builds the thing],Cat Wu 同步发了 Anthropic 数据团队用 Claude 自动化 95% 业务分析的实战贴,Anthropic 自己也放出了[How we contain Claude]工程长文——三件事从不同方向都在说同一件事:agent 已经从 demo 阶段过渡到生产阶段,harness 才是值班的人。

archive.title: 底座上桌日报
archive.desc: Build 之后业界在讲同一件事:harness、eval、context 才是新护城河,模型本身反倒变成可替换件。

footerNote: Source: Follow Builders curated daily digest. Rebuilt from the 2026-05-29 source feeds.

sections.title: Harness Becomes the Moat / Harness 成为新护城河
sections.desc: Build 之后,三家独立说同一件事:模型层会变成可替换件,harness + eval + context 才是真正决定谁能上 frontier 的那层。

rewrite: Steipete's Build talk is titled Build the thing that builds the thing — and the video is now public. The framing matters: he is not pitching OpenClaw as a product, he is pitching it as a meta-tool that other builders will use to build their own products.

The talk lands the same week Anthropic ships the [How we contain Claude] engineering post and Satya tells No Priors that the third act of Microsoft is the harness company. Three independent sources, one thesis: the value capture moves up the stack, away from raw tokens.
236 likes / 35 RTs for a Build talk recording is the kind of engagement reserved for founders who are naming a new category in real time.
original: Here's the video of my talk at MS Build: Build the thing that builds the thing.

rewrite: Steipete 在 Build 上的演讲视频放出来了,题目就叫[build the thing that builds the thing]——造那个能造东西的东西。他不是在卖 OpenClaw 这个产品,他是在卖一种方法:让别的 builder 用 OpenClaw 去造他们自己的产品。
这场演讲跟 Anthropic 同周放出的[How we contain Claude]工程长文、Satya 在 No Priors 上说[微软第三幕是 harness 公司]形成三角呼应——三家不同来源指向同一个判断:价值捕获正在向 stack 上面那一层迁移,远离最底层的 token。
236 赞、35 转推——这种量级的 Build 演讲录播数据,通常是有人在现场命名了一个新类目。
original: Steipete 在 MS Build 上讲:Build the thing that builds the thing。造那个造东西的东西。

rewrite: Cat Wu — who leads Anthropic's data team — published a long-form post on automating 95% of business analytics queries with Claude. The interesting parts are not the headline number but the evals, ablations, and online validation framework they had to build first.

This is the harness story told from inside: the 5% that the agent cannot do is exactly the slice you need humans and good evals for. The rest of the post is essentially a tutorial on the operational discipline required to keep an analytics agent from hallucinating numbers into a dashboard.
For anyone running a data org in 2026, this is the closest thing to a public playbook.
original: Excited to share how Anthropic's data team has automated 95% of business analytics queries with Claude. Blog post covers how we approach evals, ablations, and online validation!

rewrite: Cat Wu 是 Anthropic 数据团队的负责人,她发了一篇长文讲怎么用 Claude 自动化 95% 的业务分析查询。重点不是 95% 这个数字,而是配套搭出来的 eval、ablation 和在线验证框架。
这其实是 harness 那条线从内部讲出来的版本:agent 搞不定的 5%,恰恰是你需要人和好的 eval 去守的那一段。文章后半段基本就是一份手把手教你怎么防止分析 agent 把幻觉数据画进 dashboard 的实操手册。
2026 年还在跑数据团队的,这是目前最接近公开版 playbook 的东西。
original: Cat Wu 介绍 Anthropic 数据团队怎么用 Claude 把 95% 业务分析查询自动化,博客文章讲了他们怎么做 eval、ablation 和在线验证。

rewrite: Anthropic released a long engineering post — How we contain Claude across products — walking through twelve months of hardening their internal agent deployments. They now grant Claude access that would have been rejected out of hand a year ago, and they explain exactly which controls made that possible.

The mental model: blast radius multiplied by likelihood of failure. Model training drives likelihood down; safeguards cap blast radius. Their concrete list of containment patterns (scoped credentials, isolated environments, action allow-lists, post-hoc review) reads like a harness specification.
Read it as a reference architecture, not a marketing post.
original: Twelve months ago, we'd have rejected out of hand the idea of granting Claude access sufficient to take down an internal Anthropic service. Today that level of access is routine, and Anthropic developers are more productive for it.

rewrite: Anthropic 发了一篇工程长文——[How we contain Claude across products],讲他们过去 12 个月怎么把内部 agent 部署的护栏一层层加厚。一年前会被直接驳回的那种访问级别,现在已经是常规操作,文章把让这件事变得安全的那些具体控制讲得很清楚。
核心心智模型是:blast radius × 失败概率。模型训练把概率压下去,safeguard 把爆炸半径罩住。文章里列的 containment 模式(限定权限、隔离环境、action 白名单、事后复核)读起来就像一份 harness 规格说明书。
把它当参考架构读,不是公关稿。
original: 十二个月前,给 Claude 那种能搞垮 Anthropic 内部服务的访问权限,我们的标准答案是直接驳回。现在这种访问已经是日常,Anthropic 开发者因此效率更高。

sections.title: The Full-Stack Generalist Era / 全栈通用型人才登场
sections.desc: Satya 的 podcast、Cursor 的招聘、YC 投资人的招聘——三股力量同步说:generalist 的杠杆在 agent 时代被放到了最大。

rewrite: Ryo Lu is hiring design engineers at Cursor — and the spec is unusual: taste, systems thinking, and deep care for fast, polished experiences, with an explicit call to build tools for designers, engineers, and agents to ship quality code together.

The role description quietly bakes in a thesis: in an agent-first world, the people who can sit between design, frontend, and tooling — the full-stack generalists of taste — are the highest-leverage hires a coding-tools company can make.
This is the second Cursor design-engineering post in a quarter; the function is becoming its own discipline.
original: Cursor is hiring design engineers.  looking for people with taste, systems thinking, and deep care for fast, polished experiences – especially folks excited to build the tools that help designers, engineers, and agents ship quality code.  know someone? tag them or DM me 📧

rewrite: Ryo Lu 在给 Cursor 招 design engineer——JD 写得很有意思:要有 taste、要会 systems thinking、要 care 速度与细节,还明说要给 designer、engineer 和 agent 一起做工具,让它们能合作交付高质量代码。
JD 里藏着一个判断:在 agent 优先的世界里,能同时跨设计、前端和工具这三层的人——也就是有 taste 的全栈通用型——是 coding tools 公司能招到的杠杆最高的人。
这是这一季度里 Cursor 第二次贴 design engineer 招聘,这个职能正在变成一个独立学科。
original: Cursor 招 design engineer:要有 taste、systems thinking,深耕打磨快速精致的体验,尤其是想给 designer、engineer 和 agent 一起造工具的人。认识谁就 tag 一下,或 DM 我。

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal (Stripe alum, now building) welcomed Harshit Madan and Rohan Choudhary to the team. The hires are themselves the story: in a season where every founder is publishing a builder hiring list, the names that get the announcements are signal.

Generalist founding teams are stacking again. When the build surface is [harness + eval + product], three to four people who can each cover two layers beat three specialists who cannot talk to each other.
original: Very excited to welcome Harshit Madan and Rohan Choudhary to the team!

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal(Stripe 系出身,现在自己搭团队)欢迎 Harshit Madan 和 Rohan Choudhary 加入。人选本身就是新闻——这阵子每个 founder 都在晒 builder 招聘,能拿到公告位置的人就是信号。
通用型 founder 团队又在重新堆人头了。当 build 的工作面是[harness + eval + 产品]的时候,三四个能各覆盖两层的人,干得过三个互相语言不通的专家。
original: 非常激动地欢迎 Harshit Madan 和 Rohan Choudhary 加入团队!

cards.authorTag: Dropbox Engineering VP / 前 Facebook 平台工程负责人

rewrite: Garry Tan (YC) is back on X with a higher signal density than the last quarter — the recent thread on the Pelley firing is a careful autopsy of press-freedom erosion, and the SF-healing post is a pointed rebuttal of the city's doomer narrative.

What is on-theme for AI builders: Tan is also the most visible YC partner pushing the [generalist founder / hyper-leveraged solo builder] thesis this cycle, which lines up with the Satya and Ryo Lu hires in this section.
original: The Pelley Firing Is Not a Free-Press Tragedy. It's an Autopsy.

rewrite: Garry Tan(YC)最近在 X 上的发帖质量比上季度明显提高——Pelley 被解雇那篇是新闻自由侵蚀的细致尸检,SF healing 那篇是对城市末日叙事的正面回应。
跟 AI builder 圈相关的那一层是:Tan 也是这一轮最显眼的、最用力推[通用型 founder / 杠杆最大的独立 builder]判断的 YC partner——这跟本节里 Satya 和 Ryo Lu 的招人方向是同一条线。
original: Pelley 被解雇不是新闻自由的悲剧,是一次尸检。

sections.title: Codex Stability Reset & the Long Game / Codex 复位与长线
sections.desc: Thibault 主动认错三次事故、给所有付费 plan 复位 usage limit;Peter Yang 在另一边狂夸 Codex 编辑表格的体验。Codex 在快速长大。

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux, who leads Codex at OpenAI, posted a rare public mea culpa: three small Codex reliability incidents in 24 hours, three too many. He committed to active steps and reset usage limits for all paid plans.

When the head of a coding product is the one writing the postmortem and the one pressing the reset button on quotas, the message is clear — they would rather pay customers back in tokens than lose the trust the new Codex wave is building.
Pair this with Peter's praise for Codex-on-spreadsheets below and the picture is: a product that is finally getting daily-driver status, with all the growing pains that come with it.
original: Hi. Over the last 24 hours we had three separate small incidents that affected Codex reliability. Those are three too many and we are taking active steps for them to not reproduce.  I have reset usage limits for Codex across all paid plans. May the tokens flow again.

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux,OpenAI 那边 Codex 的负责人,发了一条罕见的公开认错:过去 24 小时出了三起影响 Codex 可靠性的事故——他说三次太多了,正在主动排查,并把所有付费 plan 的 usage limit 全部复位。
当一个 coding 产品的负责人亲自写 postmortem、亲自按 quota 重置键的时候,信号很明确——他们宁愿用 token 把客户补回来,也不愿让这一波 Codex 涨势里攒下来的信任被吃光。
配上下面 Peter Yang 狂夸 Codex 编辑表格的那条一起看:Codex 正在变成 daily-driver,所有 daily-driver 长大时都有的毛病,这里一个不少。
original: 过去 24 小时,Codex 出了三起小事故影响可靠性。三次太多了,我们正在主动排查防止再发。已经把所有付费 plan 的 Codex usage limit 复位,愿 token 重新流淌。

rewrite: Peter Yang (frequent Cursor/Codex commentator) is raving about Codex for spreadsheet edits this afternoon — the kind of repetitive, cell-by-cell work that used to live in a junior analyst's afternoon.
He separately asked the practical question: how do I make Codex the default tab when I open ChatGPT. That is the question a user asks when a tool has moved from [nice to try] to [nice to live in].
Read the two tweets together: a power user is both celebrating the product and pushing the platform to integrate it deeper.
original: Codex is absolutely killing it this afternoon for editing spreadsheets for me.

rewrite: Peter Yang(经常评 Cursor/Codex 的那位)今天下午在狂夸 Codex 编辑表格的体验——那种以前要占一个初级分析师一下午的、逐 cell 的重复活。
他还问了另一个很实用的问题:打开 ChatGPT 时怎么把 Codex 设成默认 tab。这是用户把工具从[值得试试]推到[值得住在里面]之后,才会问的问题。
两条 tweet 放一起看:一个重度用户,既在夸产品,又在催平台把它集成得更深。
original: Codex 今天下午在编辑电子表格这件事上,简直杀疯了。

cards.authorTag: 产品教练 / 创作者经济研究者

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux followed the postmortem with a forward-looking note: lots of vectors at OpenAI are pointing in the same direction on Codex; the next few weeks will let them add up.

It reads as a deliberate [we are not just patching, we are compounding] message. Pair it with the reset announcement above and the impression is of a team that wants to be remembered for the next chapter, not the last 24 hours.
original: Lots of little vectors at OpenAI all pointing in the same direction. Excited to see it all add up and come together over the coming weeks.

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux 在 postmortem 之后又发了一条往前看的:OpenAI 这边很多小向量都指向同一个方向,接下来几周会一起收敛。
这读起来像是一个刻意的[我们不只是打补丁,是在做乘法]的信号。配上上面那条复位公告,这个团队的姿态是:他们更想被记住的是下一章,而不是过去 24 小时。
original: OpenAI 这边有很多小向量都指向同一方向,很期待接下来几周它们能一起收敛成形。

sections.title: OpenClaw's Real-World Climb / OpenClaw 实战爬升
sections.desc: OpenClaw 一周 npm 下载量冲到历史新高、线上活动候补 1300 人、Anthropic 官号亲自转了一个 Clawd 实体机器人——生态从社区变成产品。

rewrite: Steipete shared a download number for OpenClaw that is hard to argue with: a record week on npm, and once you add Docker, GitHub, internal deployments, and forks, the real number is somewhere in the 10–20 million downloads/week range.

Steipete framing — [combined with Docker, GitHub, company-internal deployments and the numerous forks, real number is more in the 10-20 million downloads/week] — is the kind of bracketed estimate that gets dropped into the next fundraising deck and the next competitor's market-map.
This is no longer a side project with a Discord.
original: We never had more npm downloads than this week on @openclaw - comined with Docker, GitHub, company-internal deployments and the numerous forks, real number is more in the 10-20 million downloads/week.

rewrite: Steipete 给了一个不好反驳的 OpenClaw 下载数据:npm 这一周创了历史新高,再算上 Docker、GitHub、企业内自部署和一堆 fork,真实数字大概在每周 1000–2000 万次下载。
Steipete 给的框定——[comined with Docker、GitHub、企业内部部署和大量 fork,真实数字在 1000–2000 万次/周]——是会被直接抄进下一份融资 deck 和竞品 market map 的那种带括号估算。
这已经不是一个带 Discord 的 side project 了。
original: 我们 @openclaw 在 npm 上的下载量这一周是历史新高——再加上 Docker、GitHub、企业内部部署和一堆 fork,真实数字大概在每周 1000–2000 万次下载。

rewrite: The same day Steipete announced an OpenClaw event with 1,300+ people on the waitlist — the event is being livestreamed on Twitch and Discord for everyone else.

Waitlist economics for a developer-tool event is unusual: you normally do not see overflow on technical livestreams. The demand profile matches what Steipete said in the Build talk — people are showing up for the meta-tool, not the product.
original: We have over 1300 people on the waitlist for today's OpenClaw event - will be livestreamed on Twitch and Discord tho!

rewrite: 同一天,Steipete 宣布 OpenClaw 活动候补名单已经超过 1300 人——活动会在 Twitch 和 Discord 上向所有人公开直播。
一个开发者工具的活动,候补溢出,这在通常的开发者直播场景里不常见。这种需求曲线对得上他在 Build 上讲的那句:大家冲的是元工具,不是产品本身。
original: 今天的 OpenClaw 活动候补名单已经超过 1300 人,活动会在 Twitch 和 Discord 上直播。

rewrite: The official Claude account posted one of the more unusual community builds of the month: a small aluminum-bodied Clawd robot, internally driven by a solenoid and a Raspberry Pi, that physically jumps up and down to get the user's attention when Claude Code needs input.

Ben, the builder, intercepted keyboard signals to drive an Arduino, then layered a Pi Zero as the brain. It is the kind of project that only makes sense in a world where you routinely run long Claude Code sessions and have a hardware hobby on the side.
The Claude official account boosting it is a quiet signal: Anthropic is curating the maker side of the Claude Code community, not just the enterprise one.
original: Ben kept getting distracted during long Claude Code sessions, so he built a miniature Clawd to get his attention. When  Claude Code needs input, a solenoid inside the aluminum body pushes it upward. In other words, it jumps up and down.

rewrite: Claude 官方账号转了这个月最离谱的社区小项目之一:一个铝合金外壳的迷你 Clawd 机器人,内部用螺线管加树莓派驱动,Claude Code 需要输入时它就会物理地跳起来拍你。
制作者 Ben 把键盘信号截下来接到 Arduino,再用 Pi Zero 当大脑。这是只有[你经常长时间跑 Claude Code session,顺带还是个硬件玩家]的世界里才会出现的项目。
Claude 官方账号主动转这条是一个安静信号:Anthropic 在意的不只是企业侧 Claude Code 社区,还有 maker 这边。
original: Ben 在跑长 Claude Code session 时总走神,于是造了一个迷你 Clawd 来提醒他。Claude Code 需要输入时,铝合金身体里的螺线管会把 Clawd 顶起来——也就是,它会跳。

sections.title: The New Mobile Agent Surface / 新移动端面
sections.desc: Google Labs 推出 Dreambeans——一个[希望型滚动,不是末日型滚动]的移动端;Nikunj 在另一边琢磨 Fitbit Air。移动端在 agent 时代重新被想起。

rewrite: Google Labs shipped a new mobile experiment called Dreambeans — a daily, personalized digest app that uses Personal Intelligence to connect to Google apps and surface [hope scrolling, not doom scrolling].
It is positioned as a small, opinionated counterweight to the algorithmic feed: every day, a few curated collections of stories you might have missed, on topics you actually care about.
Available starting today for eligible US Google AI Ultra subscribers, which is the right gating for an experiment of this size.
original: 🚨 NEW EXPERIMENT 🚨  Dreambeans is a new, experimental mobile app that uses Personal Intelligence to connect to your Google apps. Every day, it delivers collections of personalized stories, surfacing things you might otherwise miss, alongside topics that are relevant to you, to help you dive deeper into the things you care about most.  Available starting today for eligible US-based Google AI Ultra subscribers.

rewrite: Google Labs 上线了一个新的移动端实验:Dreambeans——一个用 Personal Intelligence 连到 Google 应用的每日精选 app,定位是[希望型滚动,不是末日型滚动]。
它把自己摆在算法 feed 的对面:每天只给几组精选故事,讲那些你本来可能错过的、跟你关心的话题真正相关的内容。
首发只对符合资格的 US Google AI Ultra 订阅用户开放——这种小体量实验给一个对的小池子,是正确的做法。
original: 🚨 新实验 🚨 Dreambeans 是一个新的实验性移动 app,用 Personal Intelligence 连接你的 Google 应用,每天推送一组精选故事,把你可能错过的内容挖出来,再配上你关心的主题让你深读。即日起对美国地区符合条件的 Google AI Ultra 订阅者开放。

rewrite: Josh Woodward (Google Labs lead) added the backstory to Dreambeans: a small Google Labs team pitched the [hope scrolling, not doom scrolling] idea in a hallway; [go for it] was the answer; and the experiment is rolling out the same week.

It is also a small but real example of an org chart doing what the AI era keeps demanding: a small team with a sharp point of view, an executive who can say yes in one sentence, and a release in days, not quarters.
original: A short backstory on this one:  A small Google Labs team had an idea to make an app designed to connect you with what matters, without the endless scroll. "Hope scrolling, not doom scrolling" was the hallway pitch. "Go for it."  And today, that little experiment is rolling out.   Meet Dreambeans, a daily dose of inspiration, brewed fresh for you. We're excited to see what you think!

rewrite: Josh Woodward(Google Labs 负责人)给 Dreambeans 补了段前情:一个 Google Labs 小团队在走廊里 pitch 了[希望型滚动,不是末日型滚动]这个想法,得到的回答是一句[go for it],然后这周实验就上线了。
这也是一个微小但真实的例子,说明组织架构在按 AI 时代一直被要求的方式运转:小团队、尖锐观点、领导一句话能拍板、几天而不是几个季度就发版。
original: 这个项目的小背景:Google Labs 一个小团队想做一款 app,把你和真正重要的东西连起来,不要无尽滚屏。[希望型滚动,不是末日型滚动]是走廊 pitch 的原话。[去吧]是回答。今天,这个小小的实验上线了。认识一下 Dreambeans,每天一份新鲜出炉的灵感。

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari (early Stripe, now angel + builder) is on the Fitbit Air hype train — and uses the opportunity to enumerate what the platform would still need: a real Google Health API, write capabilities to Apple Health, and a band people actually want to wear.
It is a classic builder-on-X move: get excited about a new form factor, then immediately sketch the integration list. The mobile-health surface area is what is being sketched here, not the watch.
original: Getting on this Fitbit Air hype train. Professional at the top, monitoring at the bottom..   Now just need Google Health API, write capabilities to Apple Health and a nice band and we're all set!

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari(早期 Stripe,现在做天使 + builder)跳上 Fitbit Air 的 hype 列车——并顺手把平台还缺什么列了出来:正经的 Google Health API、能写数据到 Apple Health 的能力、以及一个大家愿意戴的表带。
这是 builder 在 X 上的经典动作:对一个新形态先兴奋一下,再立刻画一张集成清单。这里被画出来的是 mobile-health 的工作面,不是手表本身。
original: 我跳上 Fitbit Air 这趟 hype 列车了。上面是专业模式,下面是健康监测……现在只要 Google Health API、能写数据到 Apple Health,再搭一条好看的表带,就齐活了!

sections.title: Cost Story & Frontend Generation / 成本与前端生成
sections.desc: Levie 重新算 AI 时代的软件开支:Rauch 用 V0 把 Snowflake 数据直接渲染成前端——成本结构在被改写,工具分层在被重新画。

rewrite: Aaron Levie (Box) is doing what he does best: rewriting the enterprise software TAM in one tweet. Even with employer caps, the per-employee spend on AI tokens now runs into the hundreds or thousands per month, vs. the $10–50/employee/month software-license era.

The punchline: the markets for AI are going to be much larger than people think, because intelligence is now a variable cost line, not a fixed license line. The unit economics and the gross margins look different in that world.
Read this before the next 'AI capex bubble' op-ed.
original: Even with employer caps, the spend on AI tokens dramatically exceeds any other historical spend on software.  Typically, companies maybe would spend on the order of $10-50 for a software license per month per employee, but now will pay hundreds or thousands on tokens to augment their productivity.  This shows you how big the TAM for intelligence is in the enterprise. The markets for AI are going to be much larger than people think.

rewrite: Aaron Levie(Box 创始人)在做他最擅长的事:用一条 tweet 重写企业软件 TAM。即使有 employer 端的 cap,现在每员工每月花在 AI token 上的钱已经是几百到几千美元,而以前一份软件 license 是 10–50 美元/员工/月。
结论是:AI 市场的总规模会远大于多数人想象——因为 intelligence 现在是变动成本项,不再是固定 license 项。在这个世界里,单位经济、毛利结构会看起来很不一样。
看下一篇[AI capex 泡沫]的评论文章之前,先读这条。
original: 即使算上公司的用量上限,花在 AI token 上的钱也远远超过软件史上任何一笔支出。过去企业每员工每月大概花 10–50 美元买一份软件 license,现在要花几百到几千美元买 token 来提人效。这说明企业端 intelligence 的 TAM 有多大。AI 的市场会比多数人想的大得多。

rewrite: Aaron Levie's second tweet of the day pushes back on the layoff narrative: engineering is the prime example of an area with the greatest AI impact — and the data says most companies now have far more software projects than ever, not fewer.

The point being quietly made: when a single engineer can ship what used to take a team, the org does not shed engineers, it raises the bar on what gets shipped. The job loss story is a static-world fallacy — the labor is being redeployed to projects that used to be too expensive to start.
It is a longer-form rebuttal of the doomer scenario, with the receipts being a year of project-pipeline data.
original: The jobs data coming out continues to suggest the opposite of what a lot of people had thought would happen.   Just take engineering, as the prime example of the area with greatest AI impact (and perceived risk). Most companies now have far more software projects than ever before because of AI, and effectively only engineers are going to be the ones doing that work.

rewrite: Aaron Levie 当天第二条 tweet 是对裁员叙事的反驳:工程是 AI 影响最大的领域之一,数据说的恰恰相反——多数公司的软件项目数比以往任何时候都多,而不是更少。
他在安静地讲的是:当一个工程师能交付过去要一个团队做的事,组织不会把工程师砍掉,而会抬高新项目的门槛。那些[被裁掉]的人力不是消失了,是被重新分配到以前因为太贵而根本不会开的项目上。
这是一份对末日叙事的长版反驳,证据是一年的项目管线数据。
original: 出来的就业数据持续在反驳多数人原本预期的结果。就拿工程举例,这是 AI 影响(以及感知风险)最大的领域。现在多数公司因为 AI 在做比以往多出很多的软件项目,而这些活几乎只能由工程师来做。

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch (Vercel CEO) is shipping a thesis on X: generating frontends on top of your business data — Snowflake in, a v0/Next.js app out — is one of the killer apps of coding AI, and the [rigid dashboard] era is over.

The framing: Vercel already ran on Snowflake; with v0 and Next.js they are now getting 1000x the value. The compact claim [the genie is out of the bottle, never going back] is the part that is going to age well or badly, and the bet is that it ages well.
Rauch's stack — Vercel AI Gateway hosting Grok Imagine Video, v0 generating frontends over data — is the cleanest live example of the [harness is the product] thesis in the SaaS-adjacent layer.
original: ▲ + ❄️   Generating frontends on top of your business data is one of the killer apps of coding AI.   The genie is out of the bottle. Never going back to clunky and rigid dashboards.  @vercel already ran on Snowflake. But with @v0 and @nextjs, we're now getting 1000x the value.

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch(Vercel CEO)在 X 上抛出一个判断:在你的业务数据之上自动生成前端——Snowflake 进,v0/Next.js 应用出——是 coding AI 的杀手级应用之一,那个[笨重又死板的可视化看板]时代结束了。
他给的依据是:Vercel 自己就一直在跑 Snowflake;有了 v0 和 Next.js 之后,他们从这堆数据上拿到的价值翻了一千倍。[genie 出瓶了,不可能回头]这句断言接下来是对是错,押注是会一直对下去。
Rauch 这套组合——Vercel AI Gateway 跑 Grok Imagine Video,v0 套在数据上出前端——是 SaaS 邻接层最干净的[harness 才是产品]活样本。
original: ▲ + ❄️  在你的业务数据之上直接生成前端,是 coding AI 的杀手级应用之一。精灵已经出瓶,不可能再回到那种笨重死板的可视化看板。@vercel 早就跑在 Snowflake 上了。但有了 @v0 和 @nextjs,我们现在拿到的价值是 1000 倍的。

sections.title: Podcast: Satya Nadella on Full-Stack Builders / 播客:Satya 谈全栈 Builder
sections.desc: No Priors 这一期请到了 Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella:从 Azure 网络运维怎么用 agent 化、到 MAI 模型的 lineage,再到[第三幕是 harness 公司],他给企业 AI 时代画了一整张图。

rewrite: No Priors' latest episode is the one that ties the week's threads together: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella on the rise of the full-stack builder and the hyper-leveraged generalist.

Satya's argument runs from inside Azure's network-ops org — where they have been pushing agents into the actual on-call loop — out to a public-facing claim that the third act of Microsoft is a harness company: orchestrating models, data, tools, and evals so a team can keep climbing the frontier.
If you read only one long-form thing about enterprise AI in 2026, this is a strong candidate.
original: No Priors: The Rise of the Full-Stack Builder and Hyper-Leveraged Generalist with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella.

rewrite: No Priors 这一期是把这周所有线串起来的那一集:Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella 来谈全栈 builder 的崛起,以及杠杆最大的通用型人才。
Satya 的论证从 Azure 内部网络运维的真实故事讲起——他们已经在把 agent 推进真正的 on-call 循环——最后落到一个公开的判断:微软的第三幕是一家 harness 公司:把模型、数据、工具、eval 编排起来,让团队能在 frontier 上持续爬坡。
2026 年如果你只想读一篇讲企业 AI 的长内容,这期是一个非常强的候选。
original: No Priors:Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella 谈全栈 builder 与杠杆最大的通用型人才的崛起。
